questions.question: How accurate is the AI compared to a human dentist?
questions.answer: In clinical validation studies, our AI has demonstrated detection accuracy comparable to experienced dentists, with sensitivity of 91% and specificity of 94% for caries detection. For periodontal measurements, the AI achieves accuracy within 0.3mm of manual probing. However, the system is designed as a diagnostic aid, not a replacement for clinical judgment. The combination of AI analysis with dentist oversight provides the highest level of diagnostic accuracy, exceeding what either could achieve alone.

questions.question: Does the AI system comply with regulatory requirements?
questions.answer: Yes, our system has received FDA clearance as a Class II medical device for diagnostic assistance in dentistry. It complies with all relevant medical device regulations and standards for safety and efficacy. The system is also HIPAA-compliant, with comprehensive data security protocols including end-to-end encryption, secure cloud storage, and strict access controls to protect patient information.

questions.question: How does the system handle different image qualities and types?
questions.answer: The AI is trained on a diverse dataset of millions of dental images from various sensors, machines, and clinical settings. It can automatically compensate for variations in exposure, contrast, and positioning. The system works with standard digital radiographs (bitewings, periapicals, panoramic), CBCT volumes, and intraoral photographs. It can even enhance suboptimal images to improve diagnostic value, though best results come from quality original images.

questions.question: Will patients accept AI-assisted diagnosis?
questions.answer: Patient reception has been overwhelmingly positive. In surveys, 94% of patients reported increased confidence in practices using AI diagnostic tools. The visual nature of AI findings makes it easier for patients to understand their conditions, and they appreciate the thoroughness of the technology-enhanced examination. Many practices actually use the AI technology as a practice differentiator in their marketing, highlighting their commitment to cutting-edge care.

questions.question: How does the continuous learning aspect work?
questions.answer: The AI system employs a federated learning approach, where the core algorithms improve based on anonymized findings across our entire user base, while maintaining patient privacy. Your practice benefits from model improvements trained on millions of cases. Additionally, the system adapts to your practice's specific preferences over time, learning from how you interact with and modify its findings to better align with your clinical approach.

reviews.name: Dr. Rebecca Chen
reviews.comment: The AI diagnostic system has transformed our clinical workflow. We're detecting early interproximal caries that would have been missed, and the periodontal analysis saves us significant time during exams. The visualization tools have dramatically improved patient understanding and treatment acceptance. It's like having a specialist looking over your shoulder for every diagnosis.

reviews.name: Dr. James Wilson
reviews.comment: As a recent graduate, the AI system has been invaluable for building my diagnostic confidence. The detailed measurements and pathology detection help confirm my findings, and I've learned so much from the AI's analysis patterns. Patients are impressed by the technology, and it's helped establish my practice as cutting-edge despite being new to the community.

reviews.name: Dr. Maria Gonzalez
reviews.comment: The efficiency gains have been remarkable. What used to take 15-20 minutes of analysis now happens instantly. We catch more pathology, document findings more thoroughly, and communicate more effectively with patients. The system paid for itself within months through increased treatment acceptance alone, not to mention the time savings for our clinical team.
